items.q: Why is injection moulding suited to high-volume production?
items.correct: Tooling cost is high but per-unit cost is very low at scale.
items.misconception: It is the cheapest method for any quantity.
items.random_wrong: It is the only way to make plastic items.
items.why: Students think a 'good' process is good for everything. Injection moulding has expensive moulds - it's only economical when costs are spread over thousands or millions of parts.

items.q: What is the difference between ductility and malleability?
items.correct: Ductility is being drawn into wires; malleability is being hammered into sheets.
items.misconception: They mean exactly the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Ductility is for metals, malleability is for plastics.
items.why: Students treat these as synonyms because both involve deformation. Ductility = tensile (stretching into wires); malleability = compressive (rolling/hammering into sheets). Some metals excel at one but not the other.

items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.q: Which strategy keeps extra stock as a safety net?
items.why: Correct answer: JIC. “JIT” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Continuous small improvements. “One big factory rebuild” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A factory cuts storage costs from £20,000 to £8,000. How much is saved?
items.why: The real figure is 12000. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
